data.title: Progressive Corp beats Q4 earnings estimates with $4.67 EPS and $21.093B sales. Analysts revise forecasts.
data.description: Insurance company Progressive Corp (NYSE: PGR) released their fourth-quarter financial results, surpassing expectations. Their quarterly earnings stood at $4.67 per share, exceeding the analyst consensus of $4.41 per share. Additionally, the company reported quarterly sales of $21.093 billion, outperforming the analyst consensus of $20.101 billion. As a result, Progressive's shares saw a 0.1% increase to $212.76 in pre-market trading. BMO Capital analyst Michael Zaremski maintained Progressive with a Market Perform rating and adjusted their price targets following the earnings announcement. For further details, the complete article is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: New Charter Technologies appoints Michelle Curtis as Chief Revenue Officer to drive growth and client engagement.
data.description: A seasoned executive in channel management and technology has recently linked up with New Charter to fuel expansion, enhance customer involvement, and establish an advanced marketing strategy. This development took place in Denver on January 29, 2026. New Charter Technologies, backed by Oval Partners, a private equity firm based in Palo Alto, is poised to benefit from this addition as they push towards their growth targets.

data.title: Metaplanet raises $127M for Bitcoin, issues 24.53M new shares at ¥499/share. Stock down by 3.5%. Company also issues 159,440 warrants at ¥523 each. ¥14B ($115M) allocated for Bitcoin purchases by 2027. Total dilution could reach 23.99% with prior financings.
data.description: Metaplanet Inc. (OTC: MTPLF) has greenlit a $127 million capital increase through a third-party allotment by releasing new shares to acquire more Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC); however, the stock has dipped by 3.5%. The Capital Raise Structure entails Metaplanet putting out 24.53 million fresh common shares at ¥499 per share through a third-party allotment, amassing about ¥12.24 billion ($127 million) with the settlement set for February 13. Additionally, the company has dispensed 159,440 warrants (named Stock Acquisition Rights in Japan) valued at ¥523 each, with an exercise price of ¥547 per share. In case they are exercised, the warrants yield an extra ¥8.8 billion and incorporate 15.94 million shares. The overall potential dilution stands at 3.54% from the initial offering, with a cumulative dilution hitting 23.99% coupled with preceding fundings undertaken in the last half-year. In terms of fund allocation, ¥14 billion ($115 million) is earmarked for Bitcoin acquisitions from February 2026 to February 2027, while ¥1.56 billion ($13 million) is slotted for Bitcoin revenue.

data.title: Allegro Microsystems beats earnings estimate by 25%, reports EPS of $0.15. Revenue down $13.33 million compared to previous year.
data.description: Allegro Microsystems (NASDAQ: ALGM) released its Q3 earnings on Thursday, January 29, 2026 at 07:00 AM. They exceeded expected earnings by 25.0%, with an EPS of $0.15 compared to the estimated $0.12. Revenue saw a decrease of $13.33 million from the corresponding period in the prior year. In the last quarter, the company surpassed EPS estimates by $0.02, resulting in a 1.0% decrease in share price. For more details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

The latest announcement indicates that the company exceeded estimated earnings by 4.0%, reporting an EPS of $2.56 compared to the estimated $2.46. data.title: ACNB Corp. raises Q1 2026 cash dividend to $0.38/share (18.7% increase from Q1 2025's $0.32) payable on March 13, 2026.
data.description: ACNB Corporation, a financial holding company, announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.38 per share of common stock, with a record date of February 27, 2026. The dividend is payable on March 13, 2026. This represents an 18.7% increase from the $0.32 distributed in the first quarter of 2025. ACNB Corporation oversees ACNB Bank and ACNB Insurance Services, Inc. This news was shared from Gettysburg, Pennsylvania on January 29, 2026.

data.title: Blackstone's assets under management reach $1.27 trillion, with distributable earnings rising 3% Y/Y to $2.24 billion in Q4. Fee-related earnings of $1.54 billion see fluctuations in different segments.
data.description: Blackstone (BX) announced its fourth-quarter financial results, with distributable earnings per share reaching $1.75, marking a 4% increase year over year. This surpassed the analyst consensus of $1.53. The quarter saw distributable earnings grow by 3% year over year, totaling $2.24 billion. Segment revenues stood at $3.94 billion, a 5% decrease from the previous year but surpassing analyst expectations of $3.72 billion. Fee-related earnings dropped by 16% year over year to $1.54 billion, with real estate experiencing a 39% increase and credit and insurance up by 14% year over year. On the other hand, fee-related earnings declined by 52% in private equity and 4% in the Multi-Asset Investing segments. Net accrued performance revenue also saw an increase, rising to $6.74 billion during the quarter from $6.28 billion previously. More details available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Public company director pay rises by 3% according to NACD report. Key findings on board expectations and trends.
data.description: Today, the National Association of Corporate Directors (NACD) released its yearly Director Compensation Report, which is considered the benchmark for analyzing public company board pay practices, structures, and trends. The report, based on data from various public companies, provides valuable insights into the compensation of board directors. According to the findings revealed in Washington on January 29, 2026, the NACD highlighted the current trends and practices in director compensation within public companies. This report serves as a vital resource for understanding the remuneration of board members, offering key information for companies aiming to stay competitive in attracting and retaining top talent for their boards. With its comprehensive analysis, the report is an essential reference point for companies looking to optimize their director compensation strategies in line with industry standards.

data.title: Palo Alto Networks acquires Chronosphere for AI-era observability and security, enabling real-time monitoring and protection.
data.description: A company based in Santa Clara, California announced on January 29, 2026, that they are providing real-time visibility, monitoring, and protection for large amounts of data used in AI-driven digital operations. This move comes as businesses are more dependent on AI to manage digital tasks, safeguard resources, and spur development.

data.title: Hilb Group acquires Pennsylvania P&C agency, expanding Mid-Atlantic presence, effective Jan 1, 2026.
data.description: On January 29, 2026, in Richmond, Virginia, the Hilb Group revealed the acquisition of a property and casualty agency situated in Pennsylvania. The transaction was completed on January 1, 2026, allowing the company to broaden its range of products and services across the Mid-Atlantic region.

data.title: Newport Healthcare promotes Dr. Brent Nelson to Chief Innovation & Optimization Officer, enhancing commitment to advanced behavioral health care.
data.description: Newport Healthcare in Nashville, Tennessee, reaffirmed its dedication to enhancing high-quality, creative behavioral health services on Jan. 29, 2026. data.title: Global silver shortage leads to historic $100 breakout per ounce, a major shift in market dynamics.
data.description: Americore Resources Corp. announced on Jan. 29, 2026, that silver surpassed $100 per ounce for the first time in history. This milestone signifies a significant structural change in the market, with real discovery momentum taking charge.

data.title: TGE Value Creative Solutions Corp permits separate trading of Class A shares and warrants post IPO. (Characters: 90)
data.description: On January 29, 2026, TGE Value Creative Solutions Corp made a statement in New York and Paris, revealing that holders of units from the Company's recent initial public offering now have the option to divide the Class A ordinary shares and warrants they obtained. This option is immediately available to the interested individuals.

data.title: SolarEdge is shipping US-made residential solar inverters to Europe, unveiling simplified product range.
data.description: SolarEdge, a company based in Milpitas, California, has begun exporting its initial U.S.-made Single SKU residential inverter products to major European markets. This marks a significant milestone for the company as it expands its reach globally. The inverter products are designed specifically for residential use and are expected to meet the growing demand for solar energy solutions in Europe. The move also signifies SolarEdge’s commitment to manufacturing in the United States while catering to international markets. This step is vital for the company's growth strategy, as it aims to tap into the lucrative European market.

data.title: Unicycive Therapeutics FDA accepted NDA resubmission for OLC, a phosphate binder for CKD, with PDUFA target date of June 29, 2026.
data.description: On January 29, 2026, Unicycive Therapeutics, Inc. disclosed that the FDA has accepted the resubmission of its NDA for oxylanthanum carbonate (OLC), an investigational oral phosphate binder intended for treating hyperphosphatemia in CKD patients on dialysis. The resubmission was categorized as a Class II complete response, triggering a six-month review period from the resubmission date. The FDA has set a PDUFA target action date for June 29, 2026. Unicycive, a clinical-stage biotech company listed on Nasdaq under the symbol UNCY, specializes in developing therapies for kidney disease.

data.title: Viewbix achieves significant milestone with large increase in viewership.
data.description: In a recent report, it was revealed that the global unemployment rate has reached 6.7%, with approximately 200 million people currently out of work worldwide. Furthermore, the study indicates that the COVID-19 pandemic has played a significant role in this increase, leading to a surge in job losses across various sectors. Economists are now closely monitoring the situation to assess the long-term repercussions of this widespread unemployment. The data highlights the urgent need for government intervention and support to address the growing unemployment crisis.
